main.description: One observed step of any agent's work, published on msg.agent.<id>.activity by an activity bridge (pi-bus today, TASK-177; other harnesses via the TASK-151 adapter seam). Bridges a session's event stream — turns, thinking, tool calls — onto the bus so the dash/crew render a headless worker without attaching to its terminal. SIGNALS, never directs. Untrusted record; bus-stamped author is authoritative.

kind.description: The activity kind: turn_start | turn_end | tool_start | tool_end | thinking | message. turn_* bracket one agent turn; tool_* bracket one tool execution; thinking carries the agent's reasoning text for a turn; message carries the agent's assistant text for a turn.

turnIndex.description: The agent turn this activity belongs to (0-based), so a reader can group a turn's thinking + tool calls + reply.

tool.description: For tool_start / tool_end: the tool name (e.g. bash, read, edit, sextant_publish).

toolCallId.description: For tool_* : a stable id correlating a tool_start with its tool_end.

args.description: For tool_start: a short, truncated human-readable rendering of the tool arguments (e.g. the bash command). Truncated to keep the bus record small; the durable detail is the worker's own session log.

result.description: For tool_end: a short, truncated rendering of the tool result. Truncated for the same reason as args.

isError.description: For tool_end: whether the tool execution errored.

text.description: For thinking: the agent's reasoning text. For message: the agent's assistant reply text. Truncated to a sane preview length.

updated.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the worker stamped on this activity (convenience; the bus-stamped frame time is authoritative).
